JetBackup uses an encryption key to encr: JetBackup uses an encryption key to encrypt valuable data. This key will be required in DR situations as JB Config backup is encrypted. Please be advised, there is no way to restore from DR without this key, and it's important to keep this key in a secure location.
JetBackup will automatically remove thes: JetBackup will automatically remove these backups if the date these backups were CREATED is older than the number of days specified in this field.

Recommended Values: 4GB is sufficient fo: Recommended Values: 4GB is sufficient for nearly all server environments. We recommend setting no more than 50% of the available server memory to allow memory for other services/processes.

Select the number of downloads that you: Select the number of downloads that you permit to keep for each account.
Set the maximum number of concurrent bac: Set the maximum number of concurrent backup/clone tasks that may run simultaneously. JetBackup utilizes one fork per Directories Backup Job or one fork per account on an Account Backup/Clone Job. (Maximum value of 10)
Set the maximum number of concurrent ext: Set the maximum number of concurrent extension tasks that may run simultaneously. JetBackup utilizes one fork per Wordpress installation. (Maximum value of 10)
Set the maximum number of concurrent res: Set the maximum number of concurrent restore/download/reindex tasks that may run simultaneously. JetBackup utilizes one fork per restore/download task on the server. (Maximum value of 10)
Set the maximum number of concurrent sys: Set the maximum number of concurrent system tasks that may run simultaneously. JetBackup utilizes one fork per system task on the server. (Maximum value of 10)
Set the maximum time a backup\/clone for: Set the maximum time a backup/clone fork can run in hours. It will automatically abort once it reaches the set TTL.
Set the queue priority group assigned to: Set the queue priority group assigned to a directory in relation to account download & restore queue priority groups.
Set the time that backup cleanup & integ: Set the time that backup cleanup & integrity check should run in days. (Minimum value of 1 and Maximum value of 90)

This option enables control over global: This option enables control over global transaction ID (GTID) information written to the dump file, by indicating whether to add a SET @@GLOBAL.gtid_purged statement to the output. This option may also cause SET @@SESSION.sql_log_bin=0 to be written to the output that disables binary logging while the dump file is being reloaded.SET @@SESSION.sql_log_bin=0 and @@GLOBAL.gtid_purged statements require SUPER privileges which may be unavailable in environments such as Amazon RDS.
This option enables you to adjust the ma: This option enables you to adjust the maximum memory limit and applies Per Task. Each Task configured in Settings can consume memory up to the limit you configure. If the memory limit is too low, this can cause sudden/unexpected failures due to being unable to allocate sufficient memory.
This option is to establish a size thres: This option is to establish a size threshold for databases, any databases exceeding the specified size will not be included in the backup.
This option preserves existing live acco: This option preserves existing live account data and merges with the selected backup.
This option suspends the account after r: This option suspends the account after restore. Automatically selected if the account was backed up as suspended.
